fields.description:
## What
Enrich the tests overlay prompt (`src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-tests.md`) with the full
behavioral-testing standard ported from DSO: BOTH halves — the recall patterns to flag AND the
FP-discrimination guards — per the locked principle that the domain rubric lives in the OVERLAY,
never in the generic Pass-2 verifier (`code-review-verify.md` stays domain-blind). Base NOT-flag
auto-downgrade rules do not apply to test-correctness findings (a test overlay must still judge test
quality). DO THIS FIRST to prove the overlay-authoring + eval pattern before the other five
specialists (all six are children of da8a).

### Mechanism (resolves plan-review block)
These are NOT new code protocols — they are REVIEW-GUIDANCE PROSE authored into the overlay prompt
body (the `<!--volatile-->`-preceding instructions of `code-review-tests.md`), the same way the base
+ existing overlays carry their rubric. The overlay LLM applies them when emitting/withholding
findings; the generic Pass-2 verifier receives NONE of this. The eval (recall + no-fire) is the
enforcement: a no-fire case per FP-guard proves the overlay does not over-suppress-or-over-fire.

### RECALL patterns the overlay must flag (test antipatterns)
- change-detector: asserts an internal name/structure/state; would break on a behavior-preserving
  refactor (Rule-4 litmus: "would this break if internals were renamed without a behavior change?").
- tautological: asserts the mock's OWN configured return value (verifies the mock framework, not the
  code). BEFORE flagging, apply the INVERTED-TRACE disproof: mentally invert the condition under
  test; if the assertion would then fail, the test IS catching real behavior — drop the claim.
- over-mocking: mocks a module INTERNAL to the unit under test (asserts internal structure).
- under-mocking: does NOT mock an external boundary (DB/network/clock/third-party) → nondeterministic.
- source-grepping: greps/reads the SOURCE UNDER TEST as an assertion (tests text, not behavior).
- existence-only: a standalone `test -f <file>` with no structural-contract purpose (change-detector).

### FP-GUARDS the overlay must honor (the three named protocols, defined)
1. **Four-Criterion Test** — a test-coupling finding may be raised (as more than a `minor` style
   nit) ONLY when at least ONE defect criterion holds: (a) Refactoring violation — breaks on a
   safe refactor that preserves observable behavior; (b) Tautological assertion — asserts a
   test-configured mock return (subject to the inverted-trace disproof above); (c) Isolation failure
   — cross-test state / fixture pollution / order dependency / network/wall-clock leak; (d)
   Regression blindness — cannot detect a regression in the specific behavior this diff introduces.
   When NONE hold (the assertion targets observable output, exercises real code, survives refactor,
   no isolation issue), it is a philosophy disagreement → suggestion at most, not a defect.
2. **Rule-5 structural-artifact exception** — `grep`/`awk` over a NON-executable INSTRUCTION artifact
   (a `.md` skill/agent/contract/prompt, a workflow YAML, project config, a manifest/index) for a
   required heading, canonical identifier, or section is a STRUCTURAL-BOUNDARY assertion and is
   VALID — it is NOT the source-grepping antipattern (which applies ONLY to grepping SOURCE CODE
   UNDER TEST). Litmus: the grepped token is a contract iff a NON-HUMAN consumer (a parser/validator/
   downstream grep/registry/CI-runner that branches on the exact value) reads it; an LLM is NOT a
   non-human consumer, so prose/heading greps guarding wording ARE change-detectors.
3. **VALID-patterns-not-to-flag** — do NOT raise a finding on any of these (they are valid by the
   standard): (i) observable post-condition assertions (incl. when the unit is the sole producer of
   the post-condition); (ii) Rule-5 structural greps on instruction artifacts; (iii) greps of
   COMMAND OUTPUT (stdout/stderr/exit code of the executed unit); (iv) table-driven / equivalence /
   classifier tests, incl. exact-value assertions on a pure function's output that "look
   tautological"; (v) exit-code / emitted-signal assertions; (vi) mocks at the EXTERNAL boundary;
   (vii) coverage-gap demands (not blocking findings on their own).

## Files
- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-tests.md (enrich the overlay prompt body — recall + FP-guards)
- src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml (already exists from f93a; ADD recall cases
  for change-detector / over-mocking / source-grepping / existence-only, and no-fire cases for the
  Four-Criterion philosophy-disagreement, a Rule-5 instruction-artifact grep, a table-driven exact-
  value test, and a boundary mock — each a valid pattern the overlay must NOT flag).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `code-review-tests.md` overlay carries BOTH the recall patterns (change-detector, tautological
      w/ inverted-trace, over/under-mocking, source-grepping, existence-only) AND the three named
      FP-guards (Four-Criterion Test, Rule-5 structural-artifact exception, VALID-patterns-not-to-flag).
- [ ] `code-review-tests.eval.yaml` gains recall cases (new antipatterns) AND no-fire cases (one per
      FP-guard family) and validates offline (`rebar prompt eval code-review-tests` parses/loads);
      the overlay does not over-suppress valid patterns nor over-fire on them.
- [ ] The overlay remains ADVISORY; NO domain test-rubric leaks into `code-review-verify.md` (the
      generic Pass-2 verifier prompt is unchanged by this story).
